perturbation.original_text: Throughout this Agreement the following terms when capitalized shall have the following meanings: ”Confidential Information” shall include all technical and non-technical information provided by either party to th e other, including but not limited to materials generated by Khronos and by Members on behalf of Khronos, and not specifically designated as non- Confidential by the providing party, incl uding all versions and revisions of draft specifications and any passwords and minutes provided to either Participant or Khronos under this agreement. Notwithstanding the above, Confidential Information will not include any information that is (a) rightfully in the public domain other than by a breach of a duty to the disclosing party; (b) rightfully received from a third party without any obligation of confidentiality; (c) rightfully known to the receiving party without any limitation on use or disclosure prior to its receipt from the disclosing party; (d) independently developed by the receiving party; (e) generally made available to third parties by the disclosing party without restriction on disclosure; or (f ) disclosed in furtherance of an order to disclose issued by a court of proper jurisdiction; pr ovided, however, in such instance, the party having received the Confidential Information will provide prompt notice to the other party in order to facilitate that party’s legal intercession.
perturbation.changed_text: Throughout this Agreement the following terms when capitalized shall have the following meanings: ”Confidential Information” shall generally include all technical information provided by either party to th e other, including but not limited to materials generated by Khronos and by Members on behalf of Khronos, and not specifically designated as non- Confidential by the providing party, incl uding all versions and revisions of draft specifications and any passwords and minutes provided to either Participant or Khronos under this agreement. Notwithstanding the above, Confidential Information will not include any information that is (a) rightfully in the public domain other than by a breach of a duty to the disclosing party; (b) rightfully received from a third party without any obligation of confidentiality; (c) rightfully known to the receiving party without any limitation on use or disclosure prior to its receipt from the disclosing party; (d) independently developed by the receiving party; (e) generally made available to third parties by the disclosing party without restriction on disclosure; or (f ) disclosed in furtherance of an order to disclose issued by a court of proper jurisdiction; pr ovided, however, in such instance, the party having received the Confidential Information will provide prompt notice to the other party in order to facilitate that party’s legal intercession.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "shall include all technical and non-technical information" to "shall generally include all technical information," the definition of 'Confidential Information' becomes ambiguous. The removal of 'non-technical information' introduces a contradiction, because other sections may assume that non-technical information is protected, especially Section 4, which discusses the protection of all 'Confidential Information' without making a distinction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION 4.1 Confidentiality Obligation Both parties will maintain all Confidential Information in confidence with at least the same degree of care that it uses to protect its ow n confidential and propri etary information, but no less than a reasonable degree of care unde r the circumstances and will not disclose the Confidential Information except as necessary for its authorized participants to evaluate and comment thereon. Both parties will mark any copies it makes of such Confidential Information “Strictly Confidential,” or with a similar legend and shall reproduce all copyright notices and disclaime rs contained in the Confidential Information. Unless the parties agree otherwise, this obligation of confidentiality will expire two (2) years from the date of disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Confidential Information' by removing 'non-technical information' from the scope of what is included. The contradicted text, Section 4, then refers to all 'Confidential Information' without differentiating between technical and non-technical information, thus creating an ambiguity about whether non-technical information is still protected. The changed definition directly contradicts the broad application of 'Confidential Information' in Section 4.

perturbation.original_text: Unless the parties agree otherwise, this obligation of confidentiality will expire two (2) years from the date of disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless the parties agree otherwise, this obligation of confidentiality will expire five (5) years from the date of disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the confidentiality expiration from two years to five years directly contradicts the original two-year term. This change affects Section 5.9, where termination terms refer back to the confidentiality obligations outlined in Section 4. The contradiction affects the parties obligations after the termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.9 Termination Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time upon written notice to the other party. Promptly after such termination, all obligations of Participant and Khronos under this Agreement will terminate, and both parties will return or destroy all materials provided by the other party pursuant to th is Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the rights and obligations set forth in Sections 4 (Confidential Information), 5.7 (No Warranty) and 5.8 (Limitation of Liability) will survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the confidentiality expiration period from two years to five years. Section 5.9 explicitly states that obligations under Section 4 (Confidential Information) survive termination of the agreement. Since the confidentiality obligation now lasts for five years instead of two, this directly contradicts the implied effect of the termination clause in 5.9, creating a contradiction as it affects the parties obligations after the termination.

perturbation.original_text: Neither this Agreement nor any rights or obligat ions under this Agreement, in whole or in part, are assignable or otherwise transferab le by one party without the prior written approval of the other, provided however, that this Agreement may be assigned by one party to a purchaser of all, or substantially all, of that party’s business or assets, whether by merger, sale of assets, sale of stock, or otherwise with out such approval.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither this Agreement nor any rights or obligat ions under this Agreement, in whole or in part, are assignable or otherwise transferab le by one party without the prior written approval of the other, however, this Agreement can be assigned by one party to a purchaser of all, or substantially all, of that party’s business or assets, whether by merger, sale of assets, sale of stock, or otherwise with out such approval if a written notice its provided.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the word 'provided' creates a contradiction. The original text allows assignment to a purchaser without approval. The changed text creates a contradiction, requiring approval for assignment generally but then seemingly allowing it without approval if a written notice its provided. This introduces ambiguity on what is required.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 Assignment Neither this Agreement nor any rights or obligat ions under this Agreement, in whole or in part, are assignable or otherwise transferab le by one party without the prior written approval of the other, however, this Agreement can be assigned by one party to a purchaser of all, or substantially all, of that party’s business or assets, whether by merger, sale of assets, sale of stock, or otherwise with out such approval if a written notice its provided.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed assignment to a purchaser of all or substantially all of a party's business or assets without approval, due to the 'provided however' clause. The changed text removes 'provided', thereby introducing a new condition: assignment to a purchaser requires written notice. This directly contradicts the initial statement that such assignments can occur without prior written approval, creating ambiguity as to the requirements for assignment. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, and the added condition of written notice directly clashes with the initial lack of requirement for written approval, based on the explanation.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and inte rpreted in accordance with the laws of the State of California, excluding its choice of laws rules. The parties hereby agree that any dispute regarding the interpretation or valid ity of, or otherwise arising out of, this Agreement shall be subject to the exclusive ju risdiction of the California state courts of Santa Clara County, California (or, if there is fe deral jurisdiction, the Un ited States District Court for the Northern District of California, San Jose), and the parties agree to submit to the personal and exclus ive jurisdiction and ve nue of these courts.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and inte rpreted in accordance with the laws of the State of Nevada, excluding its choice of laws rules. The parties hereby agree that any dispute regarding the interpretation or valid ity of, or otherwise arising out of, this Agreement shall be subject to the exclusive ju risdiction of the California state courts of Santa Clara County, California (or, if there is fe deral jurisdiction, the Un ited States District Court for the Northern District of California, San Jose), and the parties agree to submit to the personal and exclus ive jurisdiction and ve nue of these courts.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the governing law from California to Nevada creates a direct conflict, because the parties agree to jurisdiction in California courts, however the law that is being goverened now is Nevada law. This contradiction makes it unclear which state's laws will be applied and how the California courts should interpret Nevada law.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.5 Governing Law This Agreement shall be governed by and inte rpreted in accordance with the laws of the State of Nevada, excluding its choice of laws rules. The parties hereby agree that any dispute regarding the interpretation or valid ity of, or otherwise arising out of, this Agreement shall be subject to the exclusive ju risdiction of the California state courts of Santa Clara County, California (or, if there is fe deral jurisdiction, the Un ited States District Court for the Northern District of California, San Jose), and the parties agree to submit to the personal and exclus ive jurisdiction and ve nue of these courts.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies California law governs the agreement, while the changed text specifies Nevada law. The contradicted text (which is identical to the changed text) also specifies Nevada law. This creates a direct contradiction because both the changed text and contradicted text indicate Nevada law governs, while simultaneously mandating that California courts have exclusive jurisdiction. This is a strong in-text contradiction as it is unclear which state's law applies and how California courts should interpret Nevada law.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitute the complete and exclusive statement of the agreement between the parties, and supersedes all previous agr eements, proposals, oral or written, and all other communications or understandings between the parties relating to the subject matter of this Agreement. Each party acknowledges that it has not relied up on any representation or statement not contained herein. This Agr eement shall not be modified, amended or in any way altered except by an instrument in writing signed by the Participant and Khronos hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitute the complete and exclusive statement of the agreement between the parties, and supersedes all previous agr eements, proposals, oral or written, and all other communications or understandings between the parties relating to the subject matter of this Agreement. Each party acknowledges that it has not relied up on any representation or statement not contained herein. This Agr eement shall be modified, amended or in any way altered with an email acknowledgment by the Participant and Khronos hereto.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the method of modification from requiring a 'written instrument signed by both parties' to allow amendments via 'email acknowledgment' creates an in-text contradiction. Section 5.6 originally stated that modifications must be in writing and signed; the altered text allows for email amendments, directly contradicting this earlier statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.6 Complete Agreement This Agreement constitute the complete and exclusive statement of the agreement between the parties, and supersedes all previous agr eements, proposals, oral or written, and all other communications or understandings between the parties relating to the subject matter of this Agreement. Each party acknowledges that it has not relied up on any representation or statement not contained herein. This Agr eement shall be modified, amended or in any way altered with an email acknowledgment by the Participant and Khronos hereto.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement can only be modified by a 'written instrument signed by both parties'. The changed text alters this, allowing modifications via 'email acknowledgment'. This directly contradicts the original requirement for a signed written instrument, as email acknowledgment does not fulfill this requirement. Because the Changed Text and Contradicted Text are the same location, the change made in the Changed Text directly contradicts the conditions set previously in the Original Text.
